text.source_text: इतश्च न शूद्रस्याधिकारः ; यत्सत्यवचनेन शूद्रत्वाभावे निर्धारिते जाबालं गौतम उपनेतुमनुशासितुं च प्रववृते — ‘ नैतदब्राह्मणो विवक्तुमर्हति समिधं सोम्याहरोप त्वा नेष्ये न सत्यादगाः ’ (छा. उ. ४ । ४ । ५) इति श्रुतिलिङ्गात् ॥ ३७ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: itaśca na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ ; yatsatyavacanena śūdratvābhāve nirdhārite jābālaṃ gautama upanetumanuśāsituṃ ca pravavṛte — ‘ naitadabrāhmaṇo vivaktumarhati samidhaṃ somyāharopa tvā neṣye na satyādagāḥ ’ (chā. u. 4 | 4 | 5) iti śrutiliṅgāt || 37 ||

translation.literal_translation: And for this reason too there is no eligibility for the Shudra: when truthfulness established absence of Shudra status, Gautama proceeded to initiate and instruct Jabala. This is indicated by the shruti marker: 'one who is not a brahmana could not speak thus; bring fuel, dear one, I shall initiate you - you have not departed from truth.' (37)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.37 treats the Satyakama Jabala episode as linga: pedagogical initiation follows determination of non-Shudra status.
translation.interpretive_note: Initiation-trigger evidence from the truth-test narrative.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit social-category and adhikara terminology choices in records 1.3.089-1.3.096.
